commands.review_summary:
The PR removes the early non-env SecretRef availability guard from `models.list` and adds regression coverage for file SecretRef and `secretref-managed` provider config backed by an env auth profile.

PR surface: Source -19, Tests +83. Total +64 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main returns `available: false` for the reported non-env SecretRef provider config before the auth-profile checker can prove the env-backed profile; I did not execute the failing path because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed because the open PR already contains the narrow fix and this review found no actionable patch defect.

target.title: fix(gateway): honor profile auth for SecretRef model entries
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90685.

- Remove the duplicate early SecretRef availability guard from `models.list`.
- Let the existing provider auth checker decide availability, so env-backed auth profiles can mark matching provider rows available.
- Add regression coverage for both file SecretRef objects and the literal `secretref-managed` provider marker.

## Motivation / root cause

`buildPublicModelsListEntry(...)` returned `available: false` as soon as provider config had a non-env SecretRef `apiKey`. That happened before `createModelsListProviderAuthChecker(...)` could consult runtime/env auth and read-only auth profiles.

Runtime auth can legitimately resolve the same provider through an env-backed auth profile, so model browse surfaces could show a runnable provider model as unavailable.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `models.list` no longer marks provider rows unavailable when provider config carries a non-env SecretRef marker but the provider has a usable env-backed auth profile.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout with isolated test state, a `vllm` provider using non-env SecretRef provider config, and an env-backed `vllm:env` auth profile.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx --input-type=module <focused proof script>
```

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "runtimeAuthSource": "profile:vllm:env",
  "modelsListAvailable": true
}
```

Negative proof from the same local builder path:

```json
{
  "modelsListAvailableWithoutProfile": false
}
```

Observed result after fix: when `VLLM_API_KEY` backs the selected `vllm:env` profile, `models.list` returns the catalog row with `available: true`. Without a usable profile, the same non-env SecretRef provider config still returns `available: false`.

What was not tested: no live remote provider request was sent; this verifies gateway model-browse availability against persisted local auth-profile state and runtime auth resolution only.

## Regression tests and checks

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/models.test.ts --reporter dot
pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/gateway/server-methods/models-list-result.ts src/gateway/server-methods/models.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/models-list-result.ts src/gateway/server-methods/models.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:test:src
git diff --check
```

All passed locally.

## Duplicate / overlap checks

No exact open issue or PR was found for #90685 before publishing.

Checked:

```bash
gh issue list --repo openclaw/openclaw --state all --search '"models.list" "SecretRef" "auth profile"'
gh issue list --repo openclaw/openclaw --state all --search '"secretref-managed" "models.list"'
gh issue list --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open --search '"models.list" "available:false"'
gh pr list --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open --search '90685'
gh pr list --repo openclaw/openclaw --state all --search '"modelCatalogEntryHasUnknownSecretRefAvailability"'
gh pr list --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open --search '"models-list-result.ts" "auth profile"'
gh pr list --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open --search '"models.list" "SecretRef"'
```

Open PR #90328 also touches `src/gateway/server-methods/models-list-result.ts`, but only adds model-picker runtime metadata and does not change this SecretRef/profile availability path.

## Compatibility and risk

The change narrows availability policy to the existing auth checker instead of keeping a second early SecretRef-only policy path. Non-env SecretRef-only providers remain unavailable unless an existing runtime/env/profile path can prove usable auth.
target.author: rohitjavvadi
